is_admin.description: New admin flag. Omitting it leaves the current value unchanged.

owner_id.description: The numeric ID of the user who should become the project's owner.

status.description: New account status (0=active, 1=email-confirmation required, 2=disabled, 3=locked). Omitting it leaves the current value unchanged.

auth_provider.description: Resolved auth provider name, empty for local accounts.

bot_owner_id.description: The id of the owning (human) user. Set by the server on creation; a non-zero value means this user is a bot.

created.description: A timestamp when this user was created. You cannot change this value.

email.description: The user's email address. Always empty for bot users.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this user.

is_admin.description: Whether the user is an instance admin.

issuer.description: Authentication issuer; empty or 'local' for local accounts.

name.description: The full name of the user.

subject.description: External subject identifier, for non-local accounts.

updated.description: A timestamp when this user was last updated. You cannot change this value.

username.description: The username of the user. Is always unique. For bot users it must start with the 'bot-' prefix.

token.description: The short-lived JWT auth token. Send it as a bearer token on subsequent requests.

assignees.description: The full set of users to assign to the task. This replaces the task's current assignees: users not in this list are unassigned. Pass an empty array to unassign everyone. Each user must have access to the task's project.

is_admin.description: Mark the new user as an instance admin.

language.description: IETF BCP 47 language code; must exist in taskboard.

name.description: The full name of the new user. Optional.

skip_email_confirm.description: Activate the new user immediately, skipping email confirmation.

created.description: A timestamp when this notification was created. You cannot change this value.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this notification.

name.description: The name identifying the kind of notification.

notification.description: The notification payload. Shape depends on the notification's name.

read_at.description: When the notification was marked read; zero value while unread. Set via the read flag, not written directly.

created.description: A timestamp when this notification was created. You cannot change this value.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this notification.

name.description: The name identifying the kind of notification.

notification.description: The notification payload. Shape depends on the notification's name.

read.description: Set true to mark the notification read, false to mark it unread.

read_at.description: When the notification was marked read; zero value while unread. Set via the read flag, not written directly.

location.description: Where the error occurred, e.g. 'body.items[3].tags' or 'path.thing-id'

value.description: The value at the given location

created.description: A timestamp when this file was uploaded.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this file.

mime.description: The detected mime type of the file.

name.description: The original name of the uploaded file.

size.description: The size of the file in bytes.

status.description: "OK" when the service and its dependencies are reachable.

created.description: A timestamp when this label was created. You cannot change this value.

created_by.description: The user who created this label.

hex_color.description: The color this label has in hex format.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this label.

title.description: The title of the label. You'll see this one on tasks associated with it.

updated.description: A timestamp when this label was last updated. You cannot change this value.

created.description: A timestamp when this label was created. You cannot change this value.

created_by.description: The user who created this label.

hex_color.description: The color this label has in hex format.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this label.

max_permission.description: The maximum permission the requesting user has on this label (0=read, 1=read/write, 2=admin).

title.description: The title of the label. You'll see this one on tasks associated with it.

updated.description: A timestamp when this label was last updated. You cannot change this value.

created.description: A timestamp when this label was added to the task. You cannot change this value.

label_id.description: The id of the label to associate with the task.

created.description: A timestamp when this label was created. You cannot change this value.

created_by.description: The user who created this label.

hex_color.description: The color this label has in hex format.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this label.

title.description: The title of the label. You'll see this one on tasks associated with it.

updated.description: A timestamp when this label was last updated. You cannot change this value.

oidc_logout_url.description: RP-Initiated Logout URL to redirect to for OpenID Connect sessions; empty otherwise.

message.description: A human-readable status message returned by the server.

background_blur_hash.description: A small BlurHash preview of the project background, shown until the real background loads. See https://blurha.sh/.

background_information.description: Extra information about the background (e.g. attribution). When not null, the background is available at /projects/{projectID}/background.

created.description: A timestamp when this project was created. You cannot change this value.

hex_color.description: The hex color of this project, without the leading #.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this project.

identifier.description: The unique project short identifier. Used to build task identifiers (e.g. PROJ-123).

is_archived.description: Whether the project is archived. Archived projects are read-only.

is_favorite.description: Whether the project is a favorite of the requesting user. This value is per-user and depends on who makes the call.

max_permission.description: The maximum permission the requesting user has on this project (0 = read, 1 = read/write, 2 = admin).

owner.description: The user who owns this project. Set by the server; ignored on write.

parent_project_id.description: The id of the parent project. 0 if this is a top-level project.

position.description: The position of this project when listing all projects. See the tasks.position property for how positions work.

subscription.description: The requesting user's subscription status for this project. Read-only here; use the subscription endpoints to change it. Only returned when retrieving a single project.

title.description: The title of the project. You'll see this in the overview.

updated.description: A timestamp when this project was last updated. You cannot change this value.

views.description: The views configured for this project. Managed through the project view endpoints.

duplicate_shares.description: Whether to copy the project's user, team and link shares to the duplicate. Defaults to false.

duplicated_project.description: The newly created duplicate project, populated by the server in the response.

parent_project_id.description: The id of the project under which the duplicate should be created. Omit or 0 to place the copy at the top level; you need write access to the parent.

background_blur_hash.description: A small BlurHash preview of the project background, shown until the real background loads. See https://blurha.sh/.

background_information.description: Extra information about the background (e.g. attribution). When not null, the background is available at /projects/{projectID}/background.

created.description: A timestamp when this project was created. You cannot change this value.

hex_color.description: The hex color of this project, without the leading #.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this project.

identifier.description: The unique project short identifier. Used to build task identifiers (e.g. PROJ-123).

is_archived.description: Whether the project is archived. Archived projects are read-only.

is_favorite.description: Whether the project is a favorite of the requesting user. This value is per-user and depends on who makes the call.

max_permission.description: The maximum permission the requesting user has on this project (0 = read, 1 = read/write, 2 = admin).

owner.description: The user who owns this project. Set by the server; ignored on write.

parent_project_id.description: The id of the parent project. 0 if this is a top-level project.

position.description: The position of this project when listing all projects. See the tasks.position property for how positions work.

subscription.description: The requesting user's subscription status for this project. Read-only here; use the subscription endpoints to change it. Only returned when retrieving a single project.

title.description: The title of the project. You'll see this in the overview.

updated.description: A timestamp when this project was last updated. You cannot change this value.

views.description: The views configured for this project. Managed through the project view endpoints.

created.description: A timestamp when this relation was created. You cannot change this value.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this project <-> user relation.

permission.description: The permission this user has on the project. 0 = Read only, 1 = Read & Write, 2 = Admin.

updated.description: A timestamp when this relation was last updated. You cannot change this value.

username.description: The username of the user to share with. On update and delete this comes from the URL path, not the body.

bucket_configuration.description: When the bucket configuration mode is filter, holds the title and filter of each bucket.

bucket_configuration_mode.description: The bucket configuration mode. One of none, manual or filter. manual lets you move tasks between buckets; filter creates a bucket per filter.

created.description: A timestamp when this view was created. You cannot change this value.

default_bucket_id.description: The id of the bucket new tasks without a bucket are added to. Defaults to the leftmost bucket.

done_bucket_id.description: The id of the done bucket. Tasks moved here are marked done, and tasks marked done are moved here.

filter.description: The filter query used to match tasks shown in this view. See https://taskboard.io/docs/filters.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this view. Set by the server.

position.description: The position of this view in the project's list of views. Views are sorted ascending by this value.

project_id.description: The project this view belongs to. Taken from the URL path; ignored on write.

updated.description: A timestamp when this view was last updated. You cannot change this value.

view_kind.description: The kind of this view. One of list, gantt, table or kanban.

filter.description: The filter query that decides which tasks land in this bucket. See https://taskboard.io/docs/filters.

title.description: The title of the bucket this configuration creates.

bucket_configuration.description: When the bucket configuration mode is filter, holds the title and filter of each bucket.

bucket_configuration_mode.description: The bucket configuration mode. One of none, manual or filter. manual lets you move tasks between buckets; filter creates a bucket per filter.

created.description: A timestamp when this view was created. You cannot change this value.

default_bucket_id.description: The id of the bucket new tasks without a bucket are added to. Defaults to the leftmost bucket.

done_bucket_id.description: The id of the done bucket. Tasks moved here are marked done, and tasks marked done are moved here.

filter.description: The filter query used to match tasks shown in this view. See https://taskboard.io/docs/filters.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this view. Set by the server.

max_permission.description: The maximum permission the requesting user has on this view (0=read, 1=read/write, 2=admin).

position.description: The position of this view in the project's list of views. Views are sorted ascending by this value.

project_id.description: The project this view belongs to. Taken from the URL path; ignored on write.

updated.description: A timestamp when this view was last updated. You cannot change this value.

view_kind.description: The kind of this view. One of list, gantt, table or kanban.

created.description: A timestamp when this reaction was created. You cannot change this value.

user.description: The user who reacted. Set by the server from the authenticated user; ignored on write.

value.description: The reaction itself: any UTF text up to 20 characters, e.g. an emoji.

created.description: A timestamp when this filter was created. You cannot change this value.

filters.description: The task filter query and collection options this saved filter wraps.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this saved filter.

is_favorite.description: If true, the filter shows up in the Favorites pseudo-project alongside favorite projects.

owner.description: The user who owns this filter; set by the server.

updated.description: A timestamp when this filter was last updated. You cannot change this value.

created.description: A timestamp when this filter was created. You cannot change this value.

filters.description: The task filter query and collection options this saved filter wraps.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this saved filter.

is_favorite.description: If true, the filter shows up in the Favorites pseudo-project alongside favorite projects.

max_permission.description: The maximum permission the requesting user has on this saved filter (0=read, 1=read/write, 2=admin). Filters are owner-only, so this is always 2 for a successful read.

owner.description: The user who owns this filter; set by the server.

updated.description: A timestamp when this filter was last updated. You cannot change this value.

created.description: When this session was created (login time).

device_info.description: User-Agent string captured from the login request.

id.description: The session UUID; embedded in JWTs as the sid claim.

ip_address.description: IP address captured from the login request.

last_active.description: When this session was last refreshed.

refresh_token.description: The cleartext refresh token; returned only once by the login flow, never on listing.

link_shares.description: Number of link shares across all projects.

created.description: A timestamp when this subscription was created. You cannot change this value.

entity.description: The kind of entity this subscription is for. Either project or task; derived server-side from the request path.

entity_id.description: The numeric id of the subscribed entity; taken from the request path.

id.description: The numeric id of the subscription.

assignees.description: The users assigned to this task. Read-only here; use the task-assignee endpoints to change assignments.

attachments.description: The task's attachments. Read-only here; use the attachment endpoints to add or remove them.

bucket_id.description: The bucket the task is in. Only populated when the task is accessed via a view with buckets. To move a task between buckets, the new bucket must be in the same view as the old one.

buckets.description: The task's buckets across all views. Only present when requested via the buckets expand option.

comment_count.description: The number of comments on this task. Only present when requested via the comment_count expand option.

comments.description: The task's first 50 comments. Only present when requested via the comments expand option.

cover_image_attachment_id.description: The id of the attachment used as this task's cover image, or 0 for none.

created.description: When this task was created. Set by the server; ignored on write.

created_by.description: The user who created this task. Set by the server.

done_at.description: When the task was marked as done. Set by the server; ignored on write.

hex_color.description: The task color as a hex string without the leading '#'.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this task.

identifier.description: The textual task identifier, derived from the project identifier and the task index (e.g. "PROJ-12").

index.description: The per-project task index, assigned by the server.

is_favorite.description: Whether the requesting user has favorited this task. Per-user, so it differs between callers.

is_unread.description: Whether the task is unread for the requesting user. Only present when requested via the is_unread expand option.

labels.description: The labels on this task. Read-only here; use the label-task endpoints to add or remove labels.

percent_done.description: How far the task is from done, between 0 and 1.

position.description: The task's position, saved per view. Only non-zero when the task is fetched through a view endpoint; use the task-position endpoint to change it.

project_id.description: The id of the project this task belongs to. On create it is taken from the URL; on update, setting it to a different project moves the task (requires write access to the target project).

reactions.description: Reactions on this task. Only present when requested via the reactions expand option.

related_tasks.description: Related tasks grouped by relation kind. Read-only here; use the task-relation endpoints to change relations.

repeat_after.description: The interval in seconds this task repeats. When set, marking the task done re-opens it and bumps its reminders and due date by this amount.

repeat_mode.description: How the task repeats when marked done: 0 = after repeat_after seconds, 1 = monthly (ignores repeat_after), 2 = from the current date rather than the last set date.

subscription.description: The requesting user's subscription to this task. Read-only here; use the subscription endpoints to change it. Only present when reading a single task.

time_entries_count.description: The number of time entries on this task. Only present when requested via the time_entries_count expand option.

title.description: The task title. This is what you'll see in the project.

updated.description: When this task was last updated. Set by the server; ignored on write.

created.description: A timestamp when this assignment was created. You cannot change this value.

user_id.description: The id of the user to assign to the task. The user must have access to the task's project.

created.description: A timestamp when this attachment was uploaded. You cannot change this value.

created_by.description: The user who uploaded this attachment.

file.description: Metadata of the uploaded file (name, mime type, size). The bytes are fetched from the download endpoint, not this field.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this attachment.

task_id.description: The id of the task this attachment belongs to. Taken from the URL, not the body.

code.description: taskboard numeric error code; see https://taskboard.io/docs/errors/

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem.
examples: Property foo is required but is missing.

errors.description: Optional list of individual error details

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. This value should not change between occurrences of the error.

type.description: A URI reference to human-readable documentation for the error.

allow_icon_changes.description: Whether users may change project icons.

auth.description: The authentication methods enabled on this instance.

concurrent_writes.description: Whether the configured database supports concurrent writes. False on SQLite; clients should serialize batched writes when this is false.

demo_mode_enabled.description: Whether this instance runs in demo mode (data is periodically reset).

frontend_url.description: The publicly configured frontend URL of this instance.

legal.description: Links to the instance's legal documents.

max_file_size.description: The maximum allowed upload size, as a human-readable string (e.g. 20MB).

max_items_per_page.description: The maximum number of items a paginated endpoint returns per page.

motd.description: The message of the day, shown to all users.

user_deletion_enabled.description: Whether users may delete their own account.

version.description: The taskboard version this instance runs.

bucket.description: The resolved target bucket, including its updated task count.

bucket_id.description: The bucket to move the task into. On /api/v1 this is taken from the URL; a value in the body is ignored.

project_view_id.description: The view the bucket belongs to. On /api/v1 this is taken from the URL; a value in the body is ignored.

task.description: The task as it stands after the move, reflecting any done-state change.

task_id.description: The id of the task to place in the bucket.

filter.description: The filter query to match tasks by. See https://taskboard.io/docs/filters.

filter_include_nulls.description: If true, the result also includes tasks whose filtered field is null.

order_by.description: The order for each sort_by field, either asc or desc. Defaults to asc.

s.description: A search term to match tasks by their title.

sort_by.description: The fields to sort by, for example done or priority.

author.description: The user who wrote the comment. Set from the authenticated user on create; ignored on write.

comment.description: The comment text. May contain HTML; mentions are parsed and notify the mentioned users.

created.description: A timestamp when this comment was created. You cannot change this value.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this comment.

reactions.description: The reactions on this comment, keyed by reaction value. Managed through the reactions endpoints, not by writing here.

updated.description: A timestamp when this comment was last updated. You cannot change this value.

duplicated_task.description: The newly created duplicate task, populated by the server in the response.

position.description: The task's sort position within the view, as a float so a task can be placed between any two others. To drop a task between two neighbours, set this to their midpoint. Values below the minimum spacing trigger a server-side recalculation of all positions in the view, so the stored value may differ from what you sent.

project_view_id.description: The id of the project view this position applies to. Positions are stored per view, so the same task has an independent position in each of its project's views.

task_id.description: The numeric id of the task this position belongs to. Taken from the URL; ignored in the request body.

assignees.description: The users assigned to this task. Read-only here; use the task-assignee endpoints to change assignments.

attachments.description: The task's attachments. Read-only here; use the attachment endpoints to add or remove them.

bucket_id.description: The bucket the task is in. Only populated when the task is accessed via a view with buckets. To move a task between buckets, the new bucket must be in the same view as the old one.

buckets.description: The task's buckets across all views. Only present when requested via the buckets expand option.

comment_count.description: The number of comments on this task. Only present when requested via the comment_count expand option.

comments.description: The task's first 50 comments. Only present when requested via the comments expand option.

cover_image_attachment_id.description: The id of the attachment used as this task's cover image, or 0 for none.

created.description: When this task was created. Set by the server; ignored on write.

created_by.description: The user who created this task. Set by the server.

done_at.description: When the task was marked as done. Set by the server; ignored on write.

hex_color.description: The task color as a hex string without the leading '#'.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this task.

identifier.description: The textual task identifier, derived from the project identifier and the task index (e.g. "PROJ-12").

index.description: The per-project task index, assigned by the server.

is_favorite.description: Whether the requesting user has favorited this task. Per-user, so it differs between callers.

is_unread.description: Whether the task is unread for the requesting user. Only present when requested via the is_unread expand option.

labels.description: The labels on this task. Read-only here; use the label-task endpoints to add or remove labels.

max_permission.description: The maximum permission the requesting user has on this task (0=read, 1=read/write, 2=admin).

percent_done.description: How far the task is from done, between 0 and 1.

position.description: The task's position, saved per view. Only non-zero when the task is fetched through a view endpoint; use the task-position endpoint to change it.

project_id.description: The id of the project this task belongs to. On create it is taken from the URL; on update, setting it to a different project moves the task (requires write access to the target project).

reactions.description: Reactions on this task. Only present when requested via the reactions expand option.

related_tasks.description: Related tasks grouped by relation kind. Read-only here; use the task-relation endpoints to change relations.

repeat_after.description: The interval in seconds this task repeats. When set, marking the task done re-opens it and bumps its reminders and due date by this amount.

repeat_mode.description: How the task repeats when marked done: 0 = after repeat_after seconds, 1 = monthly (ignores repeat_after), 2 = from the current date rather than the last set date.

subscription.description: The requesting user's subscription to this task. Read-only here; use the subscription endpoints to change it. Only present when reading a single task.

time_entries_count.description: The number of time entries on this task. Only present when requested via the time_entries_count expand option.

title.description: The task title. This is what you'll see in the project.

updated.description: When this task was last updated. Set by the server; ignored on write.

created.description: A timestamp when this relation was created. You cannot change this value.

created_by.description: The user who created this relation.

other_task_id.description: The id of the other task this relation points to.

relation_kind.description: The kind of relation, describing the direction from the base task to the other task (e.g. subtask, blocking, related). The inverse relation is created automatically.

task_id.description: The id of the base task. Set from the URL path; ignored in the request body.

created.description: A timestamp when this team was created. You cannot change this value.

created_by.description: The user who created this team. Set by the server.

external_id.description: The team's external id, set by the openid or ldap provider that created it. Read-only for clients.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this team.

is_public.description: Whether the team should be publicly discoverable when sharing a project. Only effective if public teams are enabled on the instance.

members.description: All members of this team. Managed through the team members endpoints, not by writing to this field.

updated.description: A timestamp when this team was last updated. You cannot change this value.

admin.description: Whether the member is an admin of the team. Team admins can add and remove members and toggle other members' admin status.

created.description: A timestamp when this member was added to the team. You cannot change this value.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this team member relation. Set by the server.

created.description: A timestamp when this relation was created. You cannot change this value.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this project <-> team relation.

permission.description: The permission this team has on the project: 0 = Read only, 1 = Read & Write, 2 = Admin.

team_id.description: The id of the team that gets access to the project.

updated.description: A timestamp when this relation was last updated. You cannot change this value.

created.description: A timestamp when this team was created. You cannot change this value.

created_by.description: The user who created this team. Set by the server.

external_id.description: The team's external id, set by the openid or ldap provider that created it. Read-only for clients.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this team.

is_public.description: Whether the team should be publicly discoverable when sharing a project. Only effective if public teams are enabled on the instance.

max_permission.description: The maximum permission the requesting user has on this team (0=read, 2=admin). Teams have no write tier.

members.description: All members of this team. Managed through the team members endpoints, not by writing to this field.

updated.description: A timestamp when this team was last updated. You cannot change this value.

bot_owner_id.description: The id of the owning (human) user. Set by the server on creation; a non-zero value means this user is a bot.

created.description: A timestamp when this user was created. You cannot change this value.

email.description: The user's email address. Always empty for bot users.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this user.

name.description: The full name of the user.

updated.description: A timestamp when this user was last updated. You cannot change this value.

username.description: The username of the user. Is always unique. For bot users it must start with the 'bot-' prefix.

created.description: A timestamp when this team was created. You cannot change this value.

created_by.description: The user who created this team. Set by the server.

external_id.description: The team's external id, set by the openid or ldap provider that created it. Read-only for clients.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this team.

is_public.description: Whether the team should be publicly discoverable when sharing a project. Only effective if public teams are enabled on the instance.

members.description: All members of this team. Managed through the team members endpoints, not by writing to this field.

permission.description: The permission this team has on the project: 0 = Read only, 1 = Read & Write, 2 = Admin.

updated.description: A timestamp when this team was last updated. You cannot change this value.

bot_owner_id.description: The id of the owning (human) user. Set by the server on creation; a non-zero value means this user is a bot.

created.description: A timestamp when this user was created. You cannot change this value.

email.description: The user's email address. Always empty for bot users.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this user.

name.description: The full name of the user.

updated.description: A timestamp when this user was last updated. You cannot change this value.

username.description: The username of the user. Is always unique. For bot users it must start with the 'bot-' prefix.

new_password.description: The new password. Max 72 bytes (a bcrypt limit), which may be fewer than 72 characters.

avatar_provider.description: The avatar provider. One of: upload, initials, ldap (synced from LDAP), openid (synced from OpenID), default.

token.description: The deletion confirmation token from the email sent by the request-deletion endpoint.

password.description: The authenticated user's password. Required for local users; ignored for users authenticated via an external provider.
The authenticated user's password. Required for local users; ignored for users authenticated via an external provider.

created.description: When the export was created.

expires.description: When the export will be automatically deleted (7 days after creation).

id.description: The id of the export file.

size.description: The size of the export file in bytes.

default_project_id.description: Project a task is filed under when created without an explicit project.

discoverable_by_email.description: If true, the user can be found when searching for their exact email.

discoverable_by_name.description: If true, this user can be found by their name or parts of it when searching.

email_reminders_enabled.description: If enabled, sends email reminders of tasks to the user.

extra_settings_links.description: Additional settings links provided by the OpenID provider. Server-controlled.

frontend_settings.description: Arbitrary settings used only by the frontend. Any JSON value; stored and returned verbatim.

name.description: The full name of the user.

overdue_tasks_reminders_enabled.description: If enabled, the user gets an email for their overdue tasks each morning.

overdue_tasks_reminders_time.description: The time the daily overdue-tasks summary is sent, as HH:MM.

timezone.description: The user's time zone, used to send task reminders in their local time.

week_start.description: The day the week starts on: 0=sunday, 1=monday, … 6=saturday.

auth_provider.description: The name of the source the user authenticated with: 'local', 'ldap', or the configured OpenID provider name.

bot_owner_id.description: The id of the owning (human) user. Set by the server on creation; a non-zero value means this user is a bot.

created.description: A timestamp when this user was created. You cannot change this value.

deletion_scheduled_at.description: When the account is scheduled for deletion, if a deletion was requested.

email.description: The user's email address. Always empty for bot users.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this user.

is_admin.description: True if the user is an instance administrator.

is_local_user.description: True if the user authenticates locally (not via LDAP or OpenID).

name.description: The full name of the user.

updated.description: A timestamp when this user was last updated. You cannot change this value.

username.description: The username of the user. Is always unique. For bot users it must start with the 'bot-' prefix.

bot_owner_id.description: The id of the owning (human) user. Set by the server on creation; a non-zero value means this user is a bot.

created.description: A timestamp when this user was created. You cannot change this value.

email.description: The user's email address. Always empty for bot users.

id.description: The unique, numeric id of this user.

name.description: The full name of the user.

permission.description: The permission this user has on the project. 0 = Read only, 1 = Read & Write, 2 = Admin.

updated.description: A timestamp when this user was last updated. You cannot change this value.

username.description: The username of the user. Is always unique. For bot users it must start with the 'bot-' prefix.

get.description: Returns per-instance counts (users, projects, tasks, teams, shares) plus the current license snapshot. Restricted to instance admins on a licensed instance; unlicensed or non-admin callers get a 404, making the endpoint indistinguishable from one that is not registered.

Returns every project on the instance, including archived ones and projects the caller does not own. Restricted to instance admins on a licensed instance; unlicensed or non-admin callers get a 404, making the endpoint indistinguishable from one that is not registered.

parameters.description: 1-based page number.
parameters.name: page

schema.description: 1-based page number.

parameters.description: Items per page (max 1000).
parameters.name: per_page

schema.description: Items per page (max 1000).

parameters.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.

patch.description: Reassigns a project to a new owner — the admin-only escape hatch the regular update endpoint does not allow. The new owner must be an active account that is not scheduled for deletion. Restricted to instance admins on a licensed instance.

parameters.description: The numeric ID of the project.
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The numeric ID of the project.

post.description: Creates a local user account, bypassing the public-registration toggle. Honours the admin-only is_admin and skip_email_confirm fields. Restricted to instance admins on a licensed instance.

delete.description: Deletes a user. With mode=now the user is removed immediately. With mode=scheduled (the default) the user is scheduled for deletion through the email-confirmation self-deletion flow. Deleting the last remaining admin is refused with 400.

parameters.description: The numeric ID of the user.
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The numeric ID of the user.

parameters.description: 'now' deletes immediately; 'scheduled' (the default) triggers the email-confirmation self-deletion flow.
parameters.name: mode

schema.description: 'now' deletes immediately; 'scheduled' (the default) triggers the email-confirmation self-deletion flow.

patch.description: Sets a user's instance-admin flag. The body field is a pointer: omitting is_admin leaves the flag unchanged. Demoting the last remaining admin is refused with 400.

parameters.description: The numeric ID of the user.
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The numeric ID of the user.

patch.description: Changes a user's account status without requiring them to log in. The body field is a pointer: omitting status leaves it unchanged. Moving the last remaining admin out of Active is refused with 400.

parameters.description: The numeric ID of the user.
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The numeric ID of the user.

get.description: Returns the user's avatar as raw image bytes. The Content-Type is chosen at runtime by the user's avatar provider (initials, an uploaded image, or the default placeholder). An unknown username is not an error — the default placeholder avatar is returned. Authenticated like every other endpoint.

parameters.description: The username of the user whose avatar to fetch.
parameters.name: username

schema.description: The username of the user whose avatar to fetch.

parameters.description: Desired avatar edge length in pixels. Clamped to the server's configured maximum if larger; providers that render fixed-size images may ignore it.
parameters.name: size

schema.description: Desired avatar edge length in pixels. Clamped to the server's configured maximum if larger; providers that render fixed-size images may ignore it.

200.description: The avatar image bytes. The Content-Type header carries the actual image type.

post.description: Creates a saved filter; the authenticated user becomes its owner. The filter query is validated before it is stored.

delete.description: Deletes a saved filter. Only the owner may delete it.

get.description: Returns a single saved filter. Only the owner may see it. Sends an ETag; pass it as If-None-Match on a later read to get a 304 Not Modified.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches one of the passed values.
parameters.name: If-Match

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches one of the passed values.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches none of the passed values. On writes, the special value * may be used to match any existing value.
parameters.name: If-None-Match

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches none of the passed values. On writes, the special value * may be used to match any existing value.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is more recent than the passed date.
parameters.name: If-Modified-Since

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is more recent than the passed date.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is older or the same as the passed date.
parameters.name: If-Unmodified-Since

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is older or the same as the passed date.

put.description: Replaces all of a saved filter's fields — only the owner may update it. Use PATCH for a partial update.

get.description: Reports whether the service and its dependencies (database) are reachable. Returns 200 with status "OK" when healthy, 500 otherwise. Public — no authentication required.
Returns version, frontend URL, motd and the enabled features of this taskboard instance. Public — no authentication required.
Returns the labels visible to the authenticated user — their own plus any used on tasks they can access. Not a global list.

post.description: Logs a user in with username and password (and a TOTP passcode when 2FA is enabled), returning a short-lived JWT. A long-lived refresh token is set as an HttpOnly cookie scoped to the refresh endpoint.
Destroys the current session server-side and clears the refresh-token cookie. A no-op for API tokens and link shares, which carry no session.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's own notifications, newest first. Link shares have no notifications and are refused.

parameters.name: page

schema.description: 1-based page number.

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.

post.description: Marks every notification of the authenticated user as read. Link shares have no notifications and are refused.

put.description: Marks one of the authenticated user's notifications as read or unread. A user can only mark their own notifications.

get.description: Returns the projects the authenticated user has access to (owned plus shared, with child projects of accessible parents), paginated. Archived projects are excluded unless is_archived=true. Pass expand=permissions to include each project's max_permission for the caller.

parameters.description: 1-based page number.
parameters.name: page

schema.description: 1-based page number.

parameters.description: Items per page (max 1000).
parameters.name: per_page

schema.description: Items per page (max 1000).

parameters.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.

parameters.description: If set to "permissions", each returned project includes the max permission the requesting user has on it (max_permission). Currently only "permissions" is supported.
parameters.name: expand

schema.description: If set to "permissions", each returned project includes the max permission the requesting user has on it (max_permission). Currently only "permissions" is supported.

parameters.description: If true, also returns archived projects.
parameters.name: is_archived

schema.description: If true, also returns archived projects.

post.description: Creates a project; the authenticated user becomes its owner. When parent_project_id is set, the caller needs write access to that parent. Default views and a backlog bucket are created automatically.

delete.description: Deletes a project together with its tasks, views, buckets and child projects. Only project admins may delete it.

get.description: Returns a single project the caller can read, including its views, the caller's favorite/subscription state and the caller's max_permission. Resolves the Favorites pseudo-project and saved-filter-backed projects. Served fresh on every call (no conditional/ETag) because the response carries user-scoped state that changes without bumping the project's updated timestamp.

put.description: Replaces a project's fields. Requires write access (admin to reparent or delete). Use PATCH for a partial update.

post.description: Deep-copies a project — its tasks, files, kanban data, assignees, comments, attachments, labels, relations and backgrounds — into a new project owned by the authenticated user. User/team/link shares are only copied when duplicate_shares is set to true. The user needs read access to the source project, plus write access to the parent project when one is given. The copy is placed under parent_project_id (top level if omitted). Returns the duplicate in duplicated_project.

parameters.description: The numeric id of the project to duplicate.
parameters.name: projectid

schema.description: The numeric id of the project to duplicate.

get.description: Returns a single task addressed by its per-project index. The {project} segment accepts either a numeric project id or a textual project identifier (e.g. "PROJ"); a value made solely of digits is always treated as an id. Embed extra, more expensive data in each task. Repeatable. One of: subtasks, buckets, reactions, comments, comment_count, time_entries_count, is_unread. Expanding can return more tasks than the page limit (subtasks) and inflate the response.

parameters.description: A numeric project id or a textual project identifier (e.g. "PROJ").

delete.description: Revokes a team's access to the project. Only project admins may remove a share.
delete.summary: Remove a team from a project

put.summary: Update a team's permission on a project

post.summary: Share a project with a user

get.description: Returns the users who can access the project — through ownership, a direct share or a team — optionally filtered by a search string. Intended for share autocomplete. Requires read access to the project.

parameters.description: Search query matched against username and name.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query matched against username and name.

get.summary: Search users with access to a project

delete.description: Revokes a user's direct access to the project, identified by username in the path. Only project admins may do this.
delete.summary: Remove a user's access to a project

put.description: Changes the permission a user has on the project; only the permission field is updated. The user is identified by username in the path. Only project admins may update a share.
put.summary: Update a user's permission on a project

See https://taskboard.io/docs/filters for the filter language.

get.summary: List a kanban view's buckets with their tasks

put.description: Moves a task into the given bucket of a project's kanban view. Requires write access to the project. Idempotent: re-sending the same bucket is a no-op. Side effects: moving a task into the view's done bucket marks it done (and out of it un-marks it); a repeating task moved into the done bucket is reopened and routed back to the default bucket instead. Moving a task into a bucket that is already at its task limit is rejected with 412. A bucket that does not resolve under the project and view in the path is rejected with 404.
put.summary: Place a task in a kanban bucket

get.description: Returns the tasks in a project view, paginated and flat. The view's own filter, sort and search are applied on top of the query. Always returns flat tasks, even for a kanban view — use the buckets endpoint to get tasks grouped by bucket. Filtering, sorting and search apply to every variant. See https://taskboard.io/docs/filters for the filter language.

delete.description: Deletes a task. Requires write access to its project.

get.description: Returns a single task by its numeric id. Sends an ETag; pass it as If-None-Match on a later read to get a 304 Not Modified. Embed extra, more expensive data in each task. Repeatable. One of: subtasks, buckets, reactions, comments, comment_count, time_entries_count, is_unread. Expanding can return more tasks than the page limit (subtasks) and inflate the response.

parameters.description: The numeric id of the task.
parameters.name: projecttask

schema.description: The numeric id of the task.

parameters.description: Embed extra data per task. Repeatable.
parameters.name: expand

schema.description: Embed extra data per task. Repeatable.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches one of the passed values.
parameters.name: If-Match

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches one of the passed values.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches none of the passed values. On writes, the special value * may be used to match any existing value.
parameters.name: If-None-Match

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches none of the passed values. On writes, the special value * may be used to match any existing value.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is more recent than the passed date.
parameters.name: If-Modified-Since

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is more recent than the passed date.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is older or the same as the passed date.
parameters.name: If-Unmodified-Since

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is older or the same as the passed date.

put.description: Replaces all of a task's fields; requires write access. Setting project_id to a different project moves the task and also requires write access to the target project. Use PATCH for a partial update.

get.description: Returns the users assigned to the given task, paginated. Requires read access to the task. Pass q to filter assignees by username.

parameters.description: 1-based page number.
parameters.name: page

schema.description: 1-based page number.

parameters.description: Items per page (max 1000).
parameters.name: per_page

schema.description: Items per page (max 1000).

parameters.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.

get.summary: List the assignees of a task

post.description: Assigns a user to the given task. The parent task is taken from the URL; the assignee is named by user_id in the body. The assignee must have access to the task's project, and the caller needs write access to the task.
post.summary: Assign a user to a task

put.description: Replaces the task's full assignee set with the users in the body: users not in the list are unassigned, new ones are added. Pass an empty array to unassign everyone. Each assignee must have access to the task's project, and the caller needs write access to the task.
put.summary: Replace all assignees of a task

delete.description: Un-assigns a user from the given task, identified by their user id in the path. Requires write access to the task.
delete.summary: Remove an assignee from a task

post.description: Copies a task — including its labels, assignees, attachments and reminders — into the same project, and records a "copied from" relation back to the original. The authenticated user needs read access to the source task and write access to its project. Returns the newly created duplicate.

parameters.description: The numeric id of the task to duplicate.
parameters.name: projecttask

schema.description: The numeric id of the task to duplicate.

get.description: Returns the labels attached to the given task, paginated. Requires read access to the task.

parameters.description: 1-based page number.
parameters.name: page

schema.description: 1-based page number.

parameters.description: Items per page (max 1000).
parameters.name: per_page

schema.description: Items per page (max 1000).

parameters.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.

get.summary: List the labels on a task

post.description: Attaches an existing label to the given task. Requires write access to the task and access to the label. Fails if the label is already on the task.
post.summary: Add a label to a task

delete.description: Detaches a label from the given task. Requires write access to the task.
delete.summary: Remove a label from a task

put.description: Clears the authenticated user's unread status for a task, dismissing the unread indicator raised by mentions and other task notifications. Idempotent: marking an already-read or inaccessible task succeeds as a no-op.

parameters.description: The numeric id of the task to mark as read.
parameters.name: projecttask

schema.description: The numeric id of the task to mark as read.

put.description: Sets where a task sorts within one of its project's views. The position is per view, so this only affects the view named by project_view_id. Requires write access to the task. Positions below the minimum spacing make the server recalculate every position in the view, so the returned value may differ from the one sent.

parameters.description: The numeric id of the task whose position to set.
parameters.name: task

schema.description: The numeric id of the task whose position to set.

put.summary: Set a task's position in a view

post.description: Relates two tasks. The authenticated user needs write access to the base task (in the path) and at least read access to the other task; the two tasks need not share a project. The inverse relation is created automatically (e.g. a subtask relation also stores the parenttask relation on the other task). Subtask/parenttask chains that would form a cycle are rejected.

parameters.description: The numeric id of the base task to relate from.
parameters.name: task

schema.description: The numeric id of the base task to relate from.

delete.description: Removes the relation identified by the base task, relation kind and other task. The automatically created inverse relation is removed as well. The authenticated user needs write access to the base task.

parameters.description: The numeric id of the base task.
parameters.name: task

schema.description: The numeric id of the base task.

parameters.description: The kind of the relation to remove.
parameters.name: relationKind

schema.description: The kind of the relation to remove.

parameters.description: The numeric id of the other task in the relation.
parameters.name: otherTask

schema.description: The numeric id of the other task in the relation.

get.description: Returns the teams the authenticated user is a member of, paginated. Set include_public=true to also surface public teams the user is not a member of, where the instance has public teams enabled.

parameters.description: 1-based page number.
parameters.name: page

schema.description: 1-based page number.

parameters.description: Items per page (max 1000).
parameters.name: per_page

schema.description: Items per page (max 1000).

parameters.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.

parameters.description: Also include public teams the user is not a member of. Only honored when public teams are enabled on the instance.
parameters.name: include_public

schema.description: Also include public teams the user is not a member of. Only honored when public teams are enabled on the instance.

post.description: Creates a team; the authenticated user becomes its first member and an admin of it.

delete.description: Deletes a team and revokes the access it granted to all of its members. Only a team admin may delete it.

get.description: Returns a single team the user is a member of. Sends an ETag; pass it as If-None-Match on a later read to get a 304 Not Modified.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches one of the passed values.
parameters.name: If-Match

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches one of the passed values.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches none of the passed values. On writes, the special value * may be used to match any existing value.
parameters.name: If-None-Match

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource matches none of the passed values. On writes, the special value * may be used to match any existing value.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is more recent than the passed date.
parameters.name: If-Modified-Since

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is more recent than the passed date.

parameters.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is older or the same as the passed date.
parameters.name: If-Unmodified-Since

schema.description: Succeeds if the server's resource date is older or the same as the passed date.

put.description: Replaces a team's fields — only a team admin may update it. Use PATCH for a partial update.

post.description: Adds a user to a team by username. Only a team admin may add members.
post.summary: Add a member to a team

delete.description: Removes a user from a team, revoking the access the team granted them. A team admin may remove anyone; a member may remove themselves. The last member of a team cannot be removed.

parameters.description: The username of the member to remove.
parameters.name: user

schema.description: The username of the member to remove.

delete.summary: Remove a member from a team

post.description: Flips the member's admin flag: an admin becomes a regular member and vice-versa. The request body is ignored. Only a team admin may do this.

parameters.description: The username of the member whose admin status to toggle.
parameters.name: user

schema.description: The username of the member whose admin status to toggle.

post.summary: Toggle a team member's admin status

get.description: Returns the authenticated user together with their settings and computed account facts (auth_provider, is_local_user, is_admin, deletion_scheduled_at).

post.description: Cancels a scheduled account deletion. Local users must provide their password.
Confirms a requested account deletion using the token from the confirmation email and schedules the account for deletion.
Starts deletion of the authenticated user's account. Local users must provide their password; a confirmation email is then sent and deletion only proceeds once confirmed.

get.description: Returns metadata about the authenticated user's current data export (id, size, creation and expiry time), or null if none has been prepared.

post.description: Streams the authenticated user's prepared data export as a zip file. Local users must confirm with their password. Fails with 404 if no export has been prepared. A POST (not GET) because the password is sent in the body.

200.description: The data export as a zip file.

post.description: Starts building a full export of the authenticated user's data. Local users must confirm with their password. The export runs in the background; an email is sent when it is ready to download.
Changes the authenticated user's password after verifying the old one. All of the user's existing sessions are invalidated.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's own active sessions, most recently active first. Never lists other users' sessions; link share tokens are forbidden.

parameters.description: 1-based page number.
parameters.name: page

schema.description: 1-based page number.

parameters.description: Items per page (max 1000).
parameters.name: per_page

schema.description: Items per page (max 1000).

parameters.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query; filters the list to items matching this string.

delete.description: Revokes a session by its UUID. Only the owning user may delete it; deleting another user's session is forbidden.

parameters.description: The UUID of the session to delete.
parameters.name: session

schema.description: The UUID of the session to delete.

put.description: Uploads an image as the authenticated user's avatar and switches their avatar provider to "upload". The image is validated to be an image, resized server-side, and stored as PNG. Replaces any previously uploaded avatar (idempotent replace, hence PUT).

avatar.description: The avatar image to upload. Must be a decodable raster image (PNG, JPEG, GIF, TIFF or BMP); it is resized server-side and re-encoded as PNG.

get.description: Returns the avatar provider configured for the authenticated user.
get.summary: Get the current user's avatar provider

put.description: Changes the avatar provider for the authenticated user. Valid values: upload, initials, ldap, openid, default.
put.summary: Set the current user's avatar provider

put.description: Sets a new email address for the authenticated user after verifying their password. If the mailer is enabled the change is pending until the user confirms it via a link sent to the new address; otherwise it takes effect immediately.
put.summary: Update the current user's email address

put.description: Replaces the authenticated user's general settings (name, reminders, discoverability, default project, week start, language, timezone, frontend settings).
put.summary: Update the current user's general settings

get.description: Returns every time zone this taskboard instance can handle. The list depends on the host system and is unsorted; sort it client-side.

post.description: Exchanges the refresh-token cookie for a new short-lived JWT. The refresh token is rotated on every call, so the previous one stops working. A new HttpOnly refresh cookie is set on the response.

get.description: Searches users by username, name or full email. Matching by name or email requires the target user to have made themselves discoverable, unless both users share an external (OIDC/LDAP) team. Email addresses are never returned.

parameters.description: Search query matched against username, name or full email.
parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query matched against username, name or full email.

get.description: Returns every reaction on the entity, grouped as a map keyed by reaction value; each value maps to the users who reacted with it. Requires read access to the entity. Not paginated.

parameters.description: The kind of entity being reacted to. Either tasks or comments (task comments).
parameters.name: entitykind

schema.description: The kind of entity being reacted to. Either tasks or comments (task comments).

parameters.description: The numeric id of the entity being reacted to.
parameters.name: entityid

schema.description: The numeric id of the entity being reacted to.

post.description: Adds the authenticated user's reaction to the entity. Requires write access. No-op if the same reaction already exists.

parameters.description: The kind of entity being reacted to. Either tasks or comments (task comments).
parameters.name: entitykind

schema.description: The kind of entity being reacted to. Either tasks or comments (task comments).

parameters.description: The numeric id of the entity being reacted to.
parameters.name: entityid

schema.description: The numeric id of the entity being reacted to.

post.description: Removes the authenticated user's own reaction from the entity. The reaction to remove is named in the body (there is no per-reaction id), so this is a POST with a body rather than a DELETE. Requires write access.

parameters.description: The kind of entity being reacted to. Either tasks or comments (task comments).
parameters.name: entitykind

schema.description: The kind of entity being reacted to. Either tasks or comments (task comments).

parameters.description: The numeric id of the entity being reacted to.
parameters.name: entityid

schema.description: The numeric id of the entity being reacted to.

post.summary: Remove a reaction from an entity
